title: 10 best herbal decoctions for numbness in hands
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for numbness in hands are a natural remedy that combines herbs, roots, and botanicals to soothe and relieve tingling sensations in the fingers. These concoctions work by increasing blood flow, reducing inflammation, and calming nerve endings, thereby alleviating numbness and discomfort. For instance, decoctions made with ginseng, turmeric, and ginger have been shown to effectively reduce numbness in hands, allowing individuals to regain sensitivity and mobility. This improved circulation and reduced numbness enables people to perform daily tasks with ease, enhancing their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with numbness in hands because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, polyphenols, and other bioactive compounds that help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants present in the decoction also help to neutralize free radicals that can cause numbness and tingling sensations. By increasing oxygen and nutrient supply to the nerves, maidenhair tree decoctions may help to alleviate symptoms of peripheral neuropathy, allowing for improved sensation and function in the hands.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds present in Maidenhair Tree decoctions help to neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve cells and contribute to numbness and tingling sensations.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Maidenhair Tree decoctions reduce inflammation in the affected areas, which can help alleviate numbness caused by conditions such as peripheral neuropathy or carpal tunnel syndrome.

Nerve-protective flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds found in Maidenhair Tree decoctions have been shown to protect nerve cells from damage and promote the regeneration of damaged nerves, helping to restore sensation and alleviate numbness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve numbness in hands by promoting blood circulation.
Barks: Contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help soothe nerve pain and numbness, providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured ginkgo biloba leaves with the boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15-20 minutes or until it reaches a comfortable temperature.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the prepared decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with numbness in hands because it contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When consumed as a decoction, curcumin is absorbed into the bloodstream and transported to areas of inflammation, such as joints and extremities like the hands. There, it reduces swelling, soothes irritation, and promotes healing, thereby alleviating numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, reduce swelling and inflammation in the hands, which can help alleviate numbness caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome or arthritis.
Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidants combat free radicals that can damage nerve cells and contribute to numbness, promoting overall nerve health and function.

Neuroprotective property: Turmeric's neuroprotective compounds, such as curcuminoids, have been shown to protect against nerve damage and degeneration, potentially helping to alleviate numbness caused by conditions like peripheral neuropathy.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric's rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Roots: The roots of turmeric are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected areas, helping to relieve numbness and weakness in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried turmeric root (curcuma longa) weighing about 500 milligrams per serving.
Grind the turmeric root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250-375 milligrams of the powdered turmeric root with 200 milliliters of boiling water in a small saucepan.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes, allowing the mixture to steep and release its active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass cup, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with numbness in hands because they improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax the blood vessels and increase blood flow to the extremities. This increased blood flow helps to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by providing oxygen and nutrients to the affected areas. Additionally, ginger's warming and relaxing effects can also help to ease muscle tension and discomfort associated with numbness.
remedy_properties: Analgesic properties: Ginger's analgesic properties help to relieve pain and discomfort associated with numbness in hands, by reducing inflammation and soothing irritated nerves.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the hands and wrists, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginger's vasodilatory effects help to increase blood flow and circulation to the hands and wrists, which can help to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations by providing more oxygen and nutrients to the affected areas.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the underground stems of ginger, known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce numbness and pain in the hands.
Barks: Ginger barks have been traditionally used to treat various types of numbness and tingling sensations, including those caused by nerve damage or compression.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried ginger root powder.
Pour boiling water over the ginger root powder.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Combine the strained liquid with an additional 1 cup.
Allow the decoction to cool and settle for at least 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper decoctions helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants present in bell peppers, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help to protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, the decoction's anti-inflammatory properties can help to relieve pressure on nerves, reducing discomfort and improving circulation. By promoting healthy blood flow and reducing inflammation, bell pepper decoctions can effectively alleviate numbness in the hands.
remedy_properties: Capsaicin-rich decoctions: Capsicum annuum (cayenne pepper or chili peppers) contain capsaicin, which can help increase blood flow and reduce numbness by stimulating the release of neurotransmitters that help regulate pain.

Ginkgo biloba extract: Ginkgo biloba is known for its ability to improve circulation and reduce numbness in extremities due to its flavonoids, which dilate blood vessels and promote healthy blood flow.

Turmeric decoctions: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric (Curcuma longa), has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate numbness in hands by reducing inflammation and improving circulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate numbness in hands by reducing swelling and promoting blood flow.
Seeds: The seeds of bell peppers contain analgesic properties that help relieve pain and numbness in the hands, making them a natural remedy for hand numbness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum leaves and stems from mature plants.
Rinse the harvested plant material with cool distilled water then pat dry.
Combine one part dried capsicum annuum leaves and stems with three parts water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced significantly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha decoctions helps with numbness in hands because of its adaptogenic properties that promote overall nervous system health. The herb's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy nerve function, ashwagandha decoctions may help to alleviate numbness and improve circulation, leading to a reduction in symptoms associated with conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome or peripheral neuropathy.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Ashwagandha helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to numbness in hands by calming the nervous system and reducing inflammation.
Antioxidant Activity: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve cells, potentially alleviating numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Neuroprotective Effects: Ashwagandha has been shown to promote neuronal health and protect against neurodegeneration, which can help to improve sensation and reduce numbness in the hands by promoting healthy nerve function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations by promoting blood circulation and nerve function.
Barks: Contains anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and discomfort caused by numbness, allowing for improved nerve conduction and transmission.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried ashwagandha root powder and 4 cups of distilled water.
Boil the water in a pot for 10 minutes then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Add the ashwagandha root powder to the boiling water and stir well for 1 minute.
Remove the decoction from heat and let it steep for 30-40 minutes then strain through cheesecloth.
Drink 1 cup of the warm decoction thrice a day for 3 months for optimal relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. The active compounds, particularly glycyrrhizin and glycyrrhetinic acid, have a vasodilatory effect, which dilates blood vessels and increases blood flow to the extremities. This enhanced circulation helps to alleviate numbness by delivering oxygen and nutrients to the hands, reducing symptoms of tingling and stiffness. Additionally, licorice's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with numbness, promoting overall hand health and function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal licorice decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected areas, which can contribute to numbness.
Antioxidant properties: Licorice root's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate numbness and tingling sensations.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and relieve numbness in the hands.

Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes are employed because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds like licoricidin and isoliquiritigenin, which can help alleviate numbness by reducing swelling and promoting blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder for every cup of water.
Combine the measured powder with two cups of distilled or filtered water in a medium-sized saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Allow the liquid to cool slightly before consuming one cup three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with numbness in hands because of its natural ability to improve blood circulation. The menthol present in peppermint stimulates the nerves, increasing blood flow and oxygen supply to the hands, which helps to alleviate numbness caused by poor circulation or nerve compression. remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured peppermint leaves and water in a medium-sized pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce it to low heat simmering.
Allow the decoction to steep for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup and let it cool.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried valeriana officinalis root and measure out one teaspoon of the herb for every cup of water.
Boil fresh water in a saucepan and add the measured amount of dried valeriana officinalis root.
Reduce heat to a simmer and allow the decoction to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.

Combine 20-30 grams of the dried st. john's wort leaves and flowers with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a large saucepan.
Reduce heat to a simmer and allow the mixture to steep for 20-25 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.

Measure out 10-15 grams of the powdered root for every liter of water to be used.

Combine ground populus balsamifera bark with 400 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container, allowing the mixture to steep for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding solids and reserving the clear decoction for further use.
Bring reserved decoction to a boil and reduce heat to simmer for an additional 15-minute period before serving it warm as needed to alleviate numbness in hands.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the feverfew plant contain bioactive compounds like parthenolide, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing numbness and pain in hands.
Buds: Feverfew buds are rich in essential oils that help to relieve numbness by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation in the affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves according to local regulations and guidelines.
Rinse the collected plant material gently with filtered water to remove dirt debris and contaminants.
Weigh out 20-30 grams of fresh tanacetum parthenium plant material or 10-15 grams of dried material for each decoction batch.
Combine the weighed plant material with 1 liter of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container and allow it to steep for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the decoction using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean container discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator at 4-8°c for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops decoctions helps with numbness in hands because it promotes blood circulation, reducing the sensation of tingling and numbness caused by poor blood flow. The anti-inflammatory properties of hops also help to reduce swelling and pressure on nerves, which can contribute to numbness. Additionally, hops has been shown to stimulate the release of neurotransmitters that promote nerve function and regeneration, further alleviating numbness and improving overall nerve health. Regular use of hop decoctions may help to alleviate chronic numbness and tingling in hands, promoting a sense of relief and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Hops decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce inflammation in the affected nerves, which may contribute to numbness or tingling sensations in the hands.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in hops decoctions help protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerve tissues, which can potentially alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Calcium-channel blocker activity: Hops decoctions contain compounds that can block calcium channels in nerve cells, which may help reduce abnormal electrical activity that can contribute to numbness or tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hops roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and numbness in the hands.
Buds: Hop buds contain a compound called humulone, which has analgesic properties that can help relieve pain and numbness in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh humulus lupulus leaves and flowers or 1 ounce of dried herb and rinse thoroughly.
Combine the harvested herb with 4 cups of purified water in a medium-sized saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half and the flavor is potent.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large glass container and discard solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature and store in the refrigerator for up to 3 days for use as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat numbness in hands with herbal decoctions is to use a blend of herbs that improve circulation and reduce inflammation. Decoctions made from ginger, turmeric, and cayenne pepper are effective in reducing numbness by increasing blood flow and soothing nerve endings. Drinking two cups of this decoction daily can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to numbness in hands that are treated with herbal decoctions are conditions like arthritis, carpal tunnel syndrome, and nerve damage. Herbal decoctions containing turmeric, ginger, and ginseng have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and numbness in the hands. Decoctions made from St. John's Wort, ashwagandha, and devil's claw can also provide relief from joint pain and stiffness associated with these conditions.
